summary: Frontend-only testing of mandatory field asterisk (*) styling consistency fix across 9 admin files. All specified files now use * for mandatory field labels. Visually confirmed via Playwright browser automation that asterisks render as rgb(239, 68, 68) (red-500). One unrelated file (ProductDetail.tsx) still has plain-text asterisk pattern but was NOT in the original fix scope.

issues: Lines 443 and 501 use {field.required && " *"} which renders a plain-text asterisk inheriting label color (NOT red). This file was NOT in the scope of the original fix. Should be changed to {field.required && *}

action_items: OPTIONAL: Fix ProductDetail.tsx lines 443 and 501 - change {field.required && " *"} to {field.required && *} to match the global pattern. This file was not in original fix scope but is a consistency issue.
NOTE: AdminDocumentsTab 'File' label - dialog is disabled (Upload Document button requires WorkDrive integration to be connected). Cannot be visually tested but code is confirmed correct at line 378.

NOTE: ArticlesTab.tsx has correct fix at line 580 but the component appears NOT to be imported/rendered anywhere in the admin panel (no admin-tab-articles in sidebar). May be dead code or needs to be wired up.
NOTE: ApiTab Try panel UI automation was not testable via Playwright due to complex endpoint row interaction, but code review confirms line 384 uses * correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: ProductDetail.tsx lines 443, 501: Remaining plain-text asterisk pattern {field.required && " *"} - not red. Should use *.
ArticlesTab.tsx: Component exists with fix applied but is NOT imported anywhere in admin. It appears to be dead code or an unused feature.

verified_fixes.ResourcesTab.tsx_line_618: VERIFIED - Price label has *, displays as rgb(239,68,68)
verified_fixes.AdminDocumentsTab.tsx_line_378: CODE VERIFIED - File label has *, dialog not accessible (WorkDrive disabled)
verified_fixes.ArticlesTab.tsx_line_580: CODE VERIFIED - Price label has *, component not rendered in admin sidebar
verified_fixes.IntakeSchemaBuilder.tsx_line_543: VERIFIED - Label field shows red asterisk rgb(239,68,68)
verified_fixes.ApiTab.tsx_line_384: CODE VERIFIED - Required params use *
verified_fixes.PartnerOrgForm.tsx_lines_222_237: CODE VERIFIED - Both dynamic label fields use conditional *
verified_fixes.CustomerSignupFields.tsx_lines_110-147: CODE VERIFIED - All address fields use conditional *
verified_fixes.Cart.tsx_lines_921-922: CODE VERIFIED - Lines use correct red span pattern
verified_fixes.UsersTab: VISUALLY VERIFIED - Full Name, Email, Role, Temp Password all show rgb(239,68,68) asterisks
verified_fixes.PartnerSubscriptionsTab: VISUALLY VERIFIED - Partner and Amount labels show rgb(239,68,68) asterisks
verified_fixes.ResourceEmailTemplatesTab: VISUALLY VERIFIED - Template Name, Description, Subject, Email Body all show rgb(239,68,68) asterisks

context_for_next_testing_agent: The mandatory asterisk styling fix is complete across all 9 specified files. All admin form labels tested visually confirmed red (rgb(239,68,68)) asterisks. One remaining issue: ProductDetail.tsx lines 443/501 still use plain-text {field.required && ' *'} pattern but was not in the original fix scope. Login flow: partner code='automate-accounts', admin email='admin@automateaccounts.local', password='ChangeMe123!', then navigate to /admin.
rca_of_issue: The original issue was that hardcoded '*' characters in label text strings inherited the label's CSS color (typically slate/grey), making them visually indistinct from the label text. The fix replaced all instances with JSX * which forces the asterisk to render in Tailwind's red-500 (#ef4444). ProductDetail.tsx was missed as it uses a dynamic pattern {field.required && ' *'} inside a dynamic form field renderer (not a standard label string).
